Hacker News Confidence

Less annoying hacker news. (What is this?)

This site scrapes Hacker News once every 30 minutes, then sorts according to this formula, with upvotes as positive signals and comments as negative ones. In my experience, the best articles to read on HN are the ones with a high upvote-to-discussion ratio. Mostly because controversial pieces tend to produce a disproportionate number of comments compared to upvotes.


  1. Book: The Emerging Science of Machine Learning Benchmarks 121 upvotes, 6 comments
  2. OpenRocket 564 upvotes, 100 comments
  3. Show HN: I built 48 lightweight SVG backgrounds you can copy/paste 271 upvotes, 53 comments
  4. Nvidia greenboost: transparently extend GPU VRAM using system RAM/NVMe 333 upvotes, 69 comments
  5. Autoresearch for SAT Solvers 126 upvotes, 24 comments
  6. Conway's Game of Life, in real life 140 upvotes, 31 comments
  7. Wander – A tiny, decentralised tool to explore the small web 283 upvotes, 72 comments
  8. 2% of ICML papers desk rejected because the authors used LLM in their reviews 17 upvotes, 1 comments
  9. Why Cloudflare rule order matters? 26 upvotes, 3 comments
  10. Cook: A simple CLI for orchestrating Claude Code 202 upvotes, 54 comments
  11. Czech Man's Stone in Barn's Foundations Is Rare Bronze Age Spearhead Mold 46 upvotes, 11 comments
  12. Show HN: Duplicate 3 layers in a 24B LLM, logical deduction .22→.76. No training 119 upvotes, 38 comments
  13. Rob Pike’s Rules of Programming (1989) 933 upvotes, 434 comments
  14. RX – a new random-access JSON alternative 89 upvotes, 38 comments
  15. LotusNotes 89 upvotes, 38 comments
  16. A sufficiently detailed spec is code 366 upvotes, 198 comments
  17. Stdwin: Standard window interface by Guido Van Rossum [pdf] 15 upvotes, 3 comments
  18. Warranty Void If Regenerated 348 upvotes, 198 comments
  19. The math that explains why bell curves are everywhere 132 upvotes, 69 comments
  20. The Serial Safety Net: Efficient Concurrency Control on Modern Hardware 5 upvotes, 0 comments
  21. Nvidia NemoClaw 315 upvotes, 212 comments
  22. CVE-2026-3888: Important Snap Flaw Enables Local Privilege Escalation to Root 137 upvotes, 87 comments
  23. Show HN: Browser grand strategy game for hundreds of players on huge maps 34 upvotes, 16 comments
  24. Eniac, the First General-Purpose Digital Computer, Turns 80 17 upvotes, 6 comments
  25. Mozilla to launch free built-in VPN in upcoming Firefox 149 133 upvotes, 90 comments
  26. Show HN: Pano, a bookmarking tool built around shareable shelves 17 upvotes, 7 comments
  27. What 81,000 people want from AI 128 upvotes, 105 comments
  28. OpenAI Has New Focus (on the IPO) 229 upvotes, 206 comments
  29. Austin’s surge of new housing construction drove down rents 540 upvotes, 638 comments
  30. Show HN: Will my flight have Starlink? 224 upvotes, 295 comments

Last generated 6:30pm, 19 March 2026. Source here.
